knowledge_gaps: Gig/contractor W-2 to 1099 reclassification flatters operating margins without changing economic costs
US rate hikes may paradoxically support domestic labor demand on net via strong dollar terms-of-trade effect (cheap imports → consumer purchasing power → sustained labor demand)
Sectoral wage dispersion (services ~4.5-5% vs. goods ~3-3.5%) creates a within-quadrant factor rotation signal where quality/low-labor-intensity factors outperform when dispersion is wide
AI displacement combined with immigration restriction creates a segmented labor market (white-collar excess, blue-collar shortage) with sticky services inflation despite stable headline unemployment
Japan's Shunto wage dynamics are the most important structural FX story in G10 — if 5%+ wage growth sustains, BoJ normalization narrows USD/JPY carry structurally
Remote work creates Dutch Disease dynamics in small EM economies where USD-denominated income bids up skilled labor costs, crowding out local tradables
Immigration impact on labor supply elasticity
Wage-price spiral risk under current conditions
